scenarios.title: Claim for battery degradation shortly after Plan purchase
scenarios.description: Customer purchased AppleCare+ seven days after buying the iPhone and now claims battery capacity has depleted by 51%.

service_fee.notes: Battery service from AppleCare+ does not require service fee for defects under section 3.1.

policy_references.snippet: Apple will service battery defects including 50% or more capacity depletion.
Plan does not cover pre-existing conditions if Plan purchased after device purchase.

scenarios.reasoning: The policy excludes coverage for pre-existing conditions under section 4.2(viii), but the battery degradation claim under 3.1 is typically covered. The key dispute is whether the 51% depletion happened before Plan purchase (excluded) or after (covered). As the Plan was purchased 7 days after the iPhone, this creates a gray area requiring further diagnostics and timestamps from Apple’s battery health logs and the proof of Plan purchase date.

scenarios.title: Battery replacement claim just before plan expiry with recent software update errors
scenarios.description: Customer reports battery capacity degraded by 52% near the end of the 24-month plan term and simultaneous iOS errors appearing after the latest OS upgrade, requesting replacement and software support under the same claim.

service_fee.notes: Battery defect repair or replacement covered at no charge under section 3.1; software support coverage ended 90 days after original purchase per section 3.3, creating tension on software issue coverage.

policy_references.snippet: Apple will service battery defects including 50% or more capacity depletion during Plan Term at no charge.
Complimentary technical support ends ninety (90) days after the Covered iPhone’s date of original purchase.
Apple may use mail-in service for hardware repairs to expedite service near plan expiry.

scenarios.reasoning: Battery defect claim is covered at no service fee per 3.1 since capacity degradation is >50% within Plan Term. However, simultaneous software errors fall outside technical support coverage as the support window expired. A judgment is required to separate claims, inform customer about unsupported software issues, and advise backup prior to hardware servicing which will erase data as per policy section 7(vi).
